evidence_quotes: I made a story post on my XXXX that my friends and I were looking for XXXX tickets to buy from anyone who happened to be selling.
I instantly took up the deal, as it was nearing the festival ( which was about a week away ), and immediately hit up my friends who were also looking for tickets, referring them to him.
Over the course of that day, XXXX and I got into group chats with my friends to facilitate the deals, promising that he would hand deliver the wristbands by that night.
I tried calling my bank ( JPMorgan Chase ), to which they said they were not liable for any transactions that were executed via XXXX willingly.

Even when XXXX told the alleged banker ( I say alleged because he is the one who got me on the phone with this person, so I don't actually know if it was a legitimate banker or not ) that he had scammed us, the bank still said it was out of their control because most of the funds had already been withdrawn and, once we had reported XXXX ( after realizing what had been done ) they had frozen his bank account completely and told him that he could only receive his money by requesting a cashier 's check.

The author initiated the deals and used online platforms (social media) to advertise and coordinate the sales. evidence_quotes: I received a text message from Bank of America asking if I attempted to make a purchase for {$170.00} Krogers with my credit card on XX/XX/XXXX at XXXX.
He stated my card had been charged with two transactions and that they were calling to verify that they were fraud since they were suspicious.
I told him no, that is not me and must be a scam.
He then told me in order to stop the wire transaction from going through I had 20 minutes to dispute this with a wire reversal.
I was told that I would likely be on hold with Bank of America for at least 40 minutes and would miss my chance to cancel the wire.
I asked to speak to a manager to ensure this was bank of America.
He then transferred me to a man who claimed to be XXXX XXXX.

They said all the right things " these calls are being monitored and recorded '' throughout the phone call and would periodically give me the time I had left to do the wire reversal ( within the 20 minutes ).
I called bank of America to see how this could have happened. I wanted to take additional steps to protect my account.
She assured me that she would file everything right away but that I needed to fill out a document and return it to them as soon as possible to avoid any delay in my case.
I was told that I should have known it was a scam because " Bank of America will never call you ''.
I locked all my bank of America credit cards on my app as well for safety.
I called XXXX XXXX to request the money be returned to my account, but they stated they could not help me and that my bank would need to contact them.
I googled to see if Bank of America had any consumer data leaked and sure enough, they did in XXXX of XXXX.
I called that same day to see if my information was leaked in that data leak.
He stated that it could have been hacked from an online retailer, but I just got the card last year and only used it a handful of times.
He said for security purposes those take a little longer.
I told him that was good then because if they didn't release the funds, I would still have a chance in getting my money back.
He even said that if the person scammed more than one person and the account was already frozen it is very likely I will get my money back and he has seen people get their money back before.
They told me I would get an email if they heard back and I would see the money in my account.
They told me if they didn't get a response by 45 business days that they would reach back out to XXXX XXXX and allow another 45 business days.
I called today XX/XX/XXXX and spoke with XXXX from the XXXX DE office who stated that my claim was closed, and they were unsuccessful and retrieving the funds from XXXX XXXX.
He stated he couldn't tell me any more information other than that.
I asked if XXXX XXXX responded and if not why bank of America was not reaching back out like they said they would multiple times and he stated that is not possible.

I asked if there was a number at the bottom of the letter, I could call to get a hold of management, or a supervisor and he stated again it was the same number that would get back to his department level.
I told him about all the misinformation I received and how I don't trust Bank of America and feel like I am being lied to.
Bank of America has 336 cases for fraud from the last 6 months and over 10 of the cases that were public are exactly like my claim.
They received calls and texts from bank of America impersonators.
They suffered the SAME HUGE LOSSES from wire transfers.
They made them the SAME PROMISES they made me.
parsed_response.rationale: The narrative details a series of fraudulent activities involving a wire transfer initiated by an impersonator posing as a Bank of America representative. The complainant received multiple calls and texts, provided personal information, and attempted to reverse the transaction. The narrative includes specific details about the wire transfer amount, the impersonator's tactics, and the various responses received from Bank of America, all of which relate to online financial transactions and transfers. The repeated attempts to resolve the issue and the conflicting information received further support the classification as an online transfer and transaction.

evidence_quotes: TD BANK APPEARS TO BE IN VIOLATION OF THEIR U.S. DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE COURT ORDERED CRIMINAL SENTENCING AGREEMENT?
I was told to send money one time from checking and savings

During my research I have uncovered evidence that strongly supports that TD Bank and its bank employees is, has and continues to allow criminals to operate within TD Bank to include what appears to be bank employees continuing to help open up accounts for malicious bad actor criminals even after TD Bank plead guilty in XXXX, and now is a convicted criminal.
TD Bank should look into what that means to violate a sentencing agreement ( see link below regarding your criminal conviction ).
CFPB Director XXXX XXXX sums it all up : This is about financial institutions fulfilling their basic obligations to protect customers money and help fraud victims recover their losses.

U.S. House and Senate clarified that the banks are to share the liability : The U.S. Protecting Consumers from Payment Scams Act ( H.R. 9303 pdf file attached ) introduced several significant amendments to the Electronic Fund Transfer Act ( EFTA ) aimed at enhancing consumer protections and ensuring greater accountability among financial institutions to include a shared liability provision wherein the financial institution holding the consumers account and the institution receiving the fraudulent transfer with discretion to include other firms that materially help facilitate the payments to share responsibility for reimbursing the consumer to encourage all parties involved to adopt more robust fraud prevention measures.

TD Bank 's criminal acts and criminal employees does not stop there as recently yet another TD Bank employee, a XXXX XXXX who worked in TD Bank 's anti-money-laundering department has pleaded guilty to a felony in connection with a check-fraud scheme and took possession of customers ' personally identifiable information.

I would like to ask why and how is it not required by law, policy or otherwise requiring banks to inform and warn customers of danger when a sending or receiving bank sends a wire / EFT 's / ACH money to any bank / institution that has a criminal record, criminal employees and/or a conviction of like kind instead of as it is now the bank/s carry on declaring to customers they are safe and secure?
parsed_response.rationale: The narrative details multiple wire transfers from TD Bank, referencing a 'rinse and repeat cycle of illegality' and the use of criminal actors. The narrative explicitly states the customer was instructed to send money from checking and savings, indicating an online transfer and transaction.
